name: Bizkaiko Bira Trail (GR 123)
description: Jog the challenging 12.5-mile Puente Bizkaia al Búnker route, featuring coastal views, the iconic Puente Bizkaia, and a 736-foot climb.
region_intro: Jogging around Getxo offers diverse coastal landscapes along the Bay of Biscay, characterized by extensive promenades, sandy beaches, and dramatic cliffs. The region features varied terrain, from flat urban paths to more challenging cliffside trails with moderate elevation changes. Natural features like the La Galea Cliffs and several beaches provide scenic backdrops for running. Getxo's mild climate supports outdoor activities throughout the year. Best jogging routes around Getxo The most popular jogging route is Ereaga Beach – Vizcaya Bridge loop from Areeta, a 6.5 miles (10.5 km) trail that takes 1 hour 5 minutes to complete, offering views of the iconic Vizcaya Bridge and Ereaga Beach. Another top favourite among local runners is Punta Galea Cliffs – Azkorri Beach View loop from Ibarbengoa, a moderate 8.3 miles (13.3 km) path that traverses the scenic Punta Galea cliffs and provides expansive views of Azkorri Beach. Local runners also love the Bizkaia Zubia/Puente Bizkaia to Punta Galea — circular, an 11.5 miles (18.6 km) trail leading through coastal areas and past the Vizcaya Bridge, often completed in about 2 hours 1 minute. Jogging around Getxo is defined by coastal paths, dramatic cliffs, and sandy beaches, with routes offering options for various ability levels. The routes in Getxo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from around 30 reviews. More than 3000 runners have used komoot to explore Getxo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Getxo?
A: Getxo offers a wide variety of running routes, with over 60 trails available for exploration. These routes cater to different fitness levels, ranging from easy coastal paths to more challenging cliffside trails.

Q: What types of terrain can I expect on Getxo's jogging paths?
A: Jogging in Getxo offers diverse terrain, from flat urban promenades and sandy beaches like Ereaga and Arrigunaga, to dramatic cliffside paths along La Galea. You'll find a mix of paved surfaces, compacted trails, and natural beach sand, providing varied experiences for runners.

Q: Are there any easy running routes suitable for beginners or a relaxed jog?
A: Yes, Getxo has several easy running options. For a relaxed experience, consider the promenade along &lt;strong&gt;Ereaga Beach&lt;/strong&gt;. While specific easy routes are not detailed here, the region offers 7 routes classified as easy, perfect for a gentle jog or beginners.

Q: Can I find challenging running trails in Getxo?
A: Absolutely. For those seeking a challenge, Getxo features 17 difficult running routes. Trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1123425528/bizkaiko-bira-trail-gr-123" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bizkaiko Bira Trail (GR 123)&lt;/a&gt; offer longer distances and more significant elevation changes, providing a demanding workout amidst stunning coastal scenery.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or natural features I can see while running in Getxo?
A: Getxo's running routes are rich with sights. You can pass by the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/527924" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vizcaya Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, a UNESCO World Heritage site, or explore the dramatic &lt;strong&gt;La Galea Cliffs&lt;/strong&gt; with their historical bunkers and lighthouse. Other natural highlights include the rugged &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/569716" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barinatxe - The Wild Beach&lt;/a&gt;, known for its unique Flysch rock formations, and the charming &lt;strong&gt;Old Port of Algorta&lt;/strong&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Getxo?
A: Yes, many of Getxo's running paths are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1123475238/bizkaia-zubia-puente-bizkaia-to-punta-galea-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bizkaia Zubia/Puente Bizkaia to Punta Galea — circular&lt;/a&gt; route, which offers a comprehensive tour of coastal areas and landmarks.

Q: Are there running routes in Getxo that are suitable for families?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' routes are not explicitly categorized, the easier and moderate coastal promenades, such as those around &lt;strong&gt;Ereaga Beach&lt;/strong&gt;, are generally suitable for families. These flatter paths offer pleasant views and are less strenuous, making them enjoyable for all ages.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails in Getxo?
A: Many of Getxo's outdoor spaces and coastal paths are dog-friendly, making it possible to enjoy a run with your canine companion. However, it's always advisable to check local regulations for specific beaches or protected areas regarding leash laws and access restrictions.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Getxo?
A: The komoot community highly rates Getxo's running trails, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 30 reviews. Runners frequently praise the stunning coastal views, the variety of terrain from beaches to cliffs, and the well-maintained paths that make for an enjoyable outdoor experience.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Getxo?
A: Getxo benefits from a mild climate, making it suitable for running year-round. However, spring and autumn offer particularly pleasant conditions with comfortable temperatures and less extreme weather. Summer can be warm but is ideal for coastal runs with a refreshing sea breeze, while winter offers cooler, crisp air, perfect for invigorating jogs.

Q: Are there any running routes that offer views of the famous Punta Galea Cliffs?
A: Yes, the dramatic &lt;strong&gt;Punta Galea Cliffs&lt;/strong&gt; are a highlight of many running routes in Getxo. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1288640" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Punta Galea Cliffs – Azkorri Beach View loop from Ibarbengoa&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent choice, providing expansive views of the cliffs and Azkorri Beach, showcasing the rugged beauty of the Bizkaia coast.
tips: Beautiful walk at the mouth of the estuary. There are good views of the estuary, Santurce, Portugalete and the Abra.
Urban beach that has a very busy promenade. The offer of restaurants and services is very varied and has a tourist office and WC.
The lighthouse is located at the entrance to the Bilbao estuary. It is a beautiful walk along the cliffs and the Cantabrian sea. There is an exercise area and benches to rest looking at the sea.
Very clean well maintained bunker with graffiti.

faqs.question: How challenging is the Puente Bizkaia al Búnker jogging route?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as a difficult or 'Hard run' and requires very good fitness due to its length of 20.1 kilometers and varied terrain. It's best suited for experienced joggers looking for a significant workout.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain will I encounter on this jogging route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily features paved surfaces, offering a generally smooth experience for jogging. You'll be running along coastal and riverside landscapes, with extraordinary views around the mouth of the Nervión River.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: The trail offers several notable sights. You'll start near the iconic Puente Bizkaia, a UNESCO World Heritage site. Other highlights include Evaristo Churruca Park, Ereaga Beach, Punta Galea Lighthouse, and the Graffiti Bunker at Liencres Cliffs.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Puente Bizkaia al Búnker route?
faqs.answer: As the route connects Portugalete and Getxo via the Puente Bizkaia, you can find parking in either town. Look for public parking areas near the bridge on either side to begin your run.

faqs.question: Is the Puente Bizkaia al Búnker route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for this exact route are not detailed, many coastal paths in the region are dog-friendly, often requiring dogs to be on a leash. It's advisable to check local signage in Portugalete and Getxo for any specific restrictions.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to use this jogging route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to jog the Puente Bizkaia al Búnker route itself. Access to the general area and the paths is free. However, if you wish to ascend the panoramic walkway of the Puente Bizkaia, there is a separate fee.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this route?
faqs.answer: The coastal nature of the route means it can be enjoyed for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures and fewer crowds. Summer can be warm, while winter might bring cooler, windier conditions, but the paved surfaces make it accessible.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. You will find yourself on sections of the Bizkaiko bira, Areetako kaia, and Azkorri bidea, among others, as you navigate the area.

faqs.question: What views can I expect along the jogging path?
faqs.answer: You'll be treated to extraordinary views throughout your run. Expect panoramic vistas of the mouth of the Nervión River, the Port of Bilbao, the surrounding mountains, and the charming towns of Portugalete and Getxo.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Puente Bizkaia al Búnker jogging route?
faqs.answer: The estimated duration for this 20.1-kilometer jogging route is approximately 2 hours and 11 minutes (7887 seconds), depending on your pace and any stops you make along the way.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for jogging with children?
faqs.answer: Given its classification as a 'difficult' and 'Hard run' requiring 'very good fitness' due to its length, the full 20.1 km route is generally not recommended for jogging with children. Shorter, more accessible sections near the Puente Bizkaia or Ereaga Beach might be more suitable for families.
